CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2025 Continued To Jun-25-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS RAMON WILLIAMS, ROLAND PICKENS, LUENNA KIM, TERRY DENTONI, EDWARD GUINA, LOUISE BROOKS HOUSTON, KARRIE JOHNSON, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, GILDA CASSANEGO, AND KATIE GAIER's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' unopposed request for judicial notice in support of its demurrer is GRANTED under California Evidence Code Section 452(c). (Fowler v. Howell (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1746, 1752.)

Defendants' demurrer is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part.

Plaintiff Igor Gruna Mocorro is suing the city and multiple hospital administrators for wrongful termination after he was pressured into resigning from his position as a nurse manager due to gross misconduct. In the operative first amended complaint, Plaintiff alleges six causes of action (COAs) for (1) discrimination - disparate treatment, against the City only, (2) harassment, (3) hostile work environment, (4) retaliation, (5) negligence and willful misconduct, and (6) intentional infliction of emotional distress.

Fourth, Fifth, and Sixth COAs against the City
"[F]ailure to allege facts demonstrating or excusing compliance with the claim presentation requirement subjects a claim against a public entity to a demurrer for failure to state a cause of action. (State of California v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 1234, 1239.) Plaintiff does not dispute noncompliance. Instead, he argues he exhausted other administrative remedies requirement to obtain a right to sue letter. (Opposition, 15.) This does not satisfy the requirement of claims presentation to the City. (Gov. Code 905, 915; S.F. Admin. Code 10.20-4.) Plaintiff does not demonstrate he can cure this deficiency. The court sustains the demurrer as to the City without leave to amend on these causes of action. The court does not address the City's remaining contentions in support of its demurrer.

Fifth and Sixth COAs against the Individual Defendants
"The procedural requirements for claim presentation are prerequisites to litigation against a local public entity or employee thereof based not only on tort liability, but on any claim for 'money or damages.' " (Gong v. City of Rosemead (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 363, 374.) Plaintiff also needed to file a government claim for the individual defendants because their liability arises from their conduct as city employees.

Plaintiff argues that these employees are sued in their individual capacities as well. This is irrelevant. Even intentional tort claims against city employees are subject to the requirement. (Gong, supra, 226 Cal.App.4th, at p. 374.) Regardless of what legal theory Plaintiff pursues, the act's requirements bar liability of the city employee individual defendants. Thus, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth and sixth causes of action against the individual defendants. The court does not consider other grounds for the demurrer as to these COAs.

Second and Third COAs against the Individual Defendants
The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to individual defendants (1) Ramon Williams, (2) Roland Pickens, (3) Luenna Kim, (4) Gilda Cassanego, (5) Karrie Johnson, and (6) Katie Gaier. The FAC does not allege any specific conduct by these defendants that pleads a claim for harassment or hostile work environment.

As to Defendants Terry Dentoni, Edward Guina, Louise Brooks Houston, the demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff points to specific adverse employment actions by those Defendants which can serve as the basis for those claims.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
